records.ety: [L. pervadere, pervasum; per + vadere to go, to walk. See Per-, and Wade.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Pervaded; p. pr. & vb. n. Pervading.]

defs: To pass or flow through, as an aperture, pore, or interstice; to permeate.
To pass or spread through the whole extent of; to be diffused throughout.
quotes: A spirit of cabal, intrigue, and proselytism pervaded all their thoughts, words, and actions.
